articles.headline: Trump says not ‘appropriate’ for Iran to participate in the World Cup in US
articles.excerpt: US president, who won the FIFA Peace Prize says Iran would be welcome but should not come for its players' safety.

articles.headline: Iran’s president sets terms to end the war: Is an off-ramp in sight?
articles.excerpt: Iran is asking for 'reparations' and a guarantee that the US and Israel will not attack in the future.

articles.headline: US military ‘not ready’ to escort oil ships through Hormuz, official says

articles.headline: Iran’s new supreme leader issues first statement
articles.excerpt: Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has issued his first statement since being appointed.

articles.headline: Pakistan’s Abrar signed by India-owned Sunrisers in Hundred auction
articles.excerpt: Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed has been signed by Indian-owned Sunrisers Leeds in UK cricket's Hundred auction.

articles.headline: Video shows moment Iran strikes US-owned oil tanker near Iraq

articles.excerpt: UN refugee agency says forced displacement likely to increase as US and Israel continue deadly strikes across Iran.

articles.excerpt: Explosions are heard for a 13th day in Iran, Israel and several Middle Eastern states.

articles.headline: Israeli attack hits civilians at Beirut beachfront: What we know
articles.excerpt: An Israeli strike on Beirut’s seafront killed at least 8 people and injured dozens more.

articles.excerpt: The use of RAF Fairford by US forces highlights the UK's increasingly active role in the US-Israeli war on Iran.

articles.headline: 'What if we're left with ruins?': Doubts creep in for Iranians who…
articles.excerpt: Teachers, engineers and shopkeepers tell the BBC they fear Iran falling into chaos.

articles.headline: Rapper-politician Balendra Shah's party wins Nepal election
articles.excerpt: The Rastriya Swatatantra Party has won a large majority in an election dominated by issues including corruption.

articles.headline: Epstein used modelling agent to recruit girls, Brazilian women tell BBC
articles.excerpt: Modelling agent used businesses to recruit girls and arrange US visas to visit Jeffrey Epstein, Brazilian women tell BBC.

articles.headline: Nineteen jailed over deadly Moscow concert attack
articles.excerpt: Gunmen opened fire at Crocus City Concert hall near Moscow in 2024, killing 149 and injuring more than 500.

articles.headline: Noma head chef resigns from restaurant amid abuse allegations
articles.excerpt: René Redzepi had previously apologised after ex-employees of the Danish restaurant accused him of creating a toxic work environment.

articles.headline: Cartoonist freed after 15 years in prison without charge in Eritrea
articles.excerpt: Biniam Solomon was known for his witty and critical cartoons before the free press was closed.

articles.headline: Chinese national arrested over attempt to smuggle 2,000 queen ants from…
articles.excerpt: The suspect had packed some ants in test tubes while others were concealed in tissue paper rolls, prosecutor says.

articles.headline: Death toll from Ethiopia floods and landslides rises to more than 100
articles.excerpt: Forecasters say storms are becoming more intense in the region, partly due to global warming.

articles.headline: Why Namibia's green energy dream could be a red flag for penguins
articles.excerpt: A near pristine desert and coastal wilderness in Namibia could soon host a huge hydrogen production facility.

articles.headline: One house, two faiths, one fasting season
articles.excerpt: Ramadan and Lent haven't overlapped since 1993 and couples like Olanrewaju and Kaosara in Nigeria are observing them together.

articles.headline: The Mugabe family after losing power - arrests, accusations and arguments
articles.excerpt: Bellarmine Mugabe will negotiate a plea deal after being accused of attempted murder and other charges in South Africa.

articles.headline: France's ghost car scandal that allowed one million illegal vehicles onto…
articles.excerpt: Fake dealerships were manipulating the state vehicle licensing agency's official records, France's auditor has found.

articles.headline: Parents accused of hiding daughter's HIV infection and neglect
articles.excerpt: The child did not see a doctor until she was six - and had not had any vaccinations, Italian media say.

articles.headline: Jo Malone sued for using her own name in collaboration with Zara
articles.excerpt: The perfumier sold the rights to her name in 1999 but has previously said she regretted the move.

articles.headline: US launches probe into trading partners including the EU, China and India
articles.excerpt: The move comes weeks after the US Supreme Court struck down a key part of Trump's tariffs policies.

articles.headline: Ukraine says it has hit Russian 'missile component' plant
articles.excerpt: Russia says the attack hit civilians and could not have been carried out without British help.

articles.headline: Most underrated player on the planet? Valverde steps up for Real

articles.headline: No evidence Swiss bus fire was terrorism, officials say
articles.excerpt: Initial reports suggest a man with "psychological problems" set himself on fire on the bus, according to police.

articles.headline: Questions mount for Hegseth over possible US involvement in strike on…
articles.excerpt: Democrats have written to ask the defence secretary whether the US was responsible, but he says the issue is being investigated.

articles.headline: Customer sues Costco for tariff refunds
articles.excerpt: The lawsuit is an indication of the complexities looming over a potential $166bn in tariff refunds.

articles.headline: A small US grocer is calling out the lower prices at big chains
articles.excerpt: It is 'impossible for us to compete', says the boss of a New York grocery store.

articles.headline: Sly fox sneaks on to cargo ship in Southampton and arrives in New York
articles.excerpt: The animal that stowed away on the vessel is now in the care of the Bronx Zoo with veterinary staff.

articles.headline: US inflation stable ahead of Iran shock
articles.excerpt: With energy prices jumping, analysts say the report might be viewed as a "historical artefact".

articles.headline: Three brothers arrested after explosion at US embassy in Oslo
articles.excerpt: Norway's police attorney says the authorities are investigating whether a foreign state actor was involved.

articles.headline: What on earth is going on with the oil price?

articles.headline: Lloyds, Bank of Scotland and Halifax apps showed customers other users'…
articles.excerpt: The Lloyds Banking Group customers reported being able to view payments and charges from other sources.

articles.headline: How Iran war laid bare the world's reliance on Gulf oil and gas
articles.excerpt: Countries around the world are feeling the impact of the conflict and the resulting energy price shock.

articles.headline: Oil price jumps despite deal to release record amount of reserves
articles.excerpt: It comes as Iranian attacks on ships intensify in the crucial Strait of Hormuz waterway.

articles.headline: John Lewis to pay first staff bonus for four years
articles.excerpt: The department store and Waitrose supermarket owner will give workers a bonus equivalent to an extra week's pay.

articles.headline: What role has cyber warfare played in Iran?
articles.excerpt: Militaries are often cagey about their cyber activities. But the US has hinted at the role it has played.

articles.headline: Is Bitcoin price at risk if private credit breaks?
articles.excerpt: A looming private credit crisis risked triggering liquidity crunches that could initially suppress Bitcoin prices, but Fed interventions may ignite a major BTC rally.

articles.headline: Bitcoin catching up to gold hints at an ‘opportunity within risk’
articles.excerpt: Bitcoin showed early signs of overtaking gold in the market as new data outlined an opportunity based on historical returns around the US midterm elections.

articles.headline: Ark Invest sees one-third of Bitcoin supply at risk from quantum threat
articles.excerpt: Ark and Unchained say about one-third of the Bitcoin supply remains exposed to future quantum threats, though the risk is still years away.

articles.headline: Bitcoin hugs $70K range as March Fed rate cut odds fall below 1%
articles.excerpt: Bitcoin reacted in kind to calm US macro data, while oil stayed volatile amid uncertainty over the duration of the Middle East conflict.

articles.headline: Binance says US midterms could boost Bitcoin and stocks
articles.excerpt: Binance Research says US midterms could set up a rebound for Bitcoin and stocks, though oil shocks and Middle East tensions may weigh near term.

articles.headline: Why Ray Dalio says Bitcoin can’t replace gold
articles.excerpt: Ray Dalio argues Bitcoin cannot replace gold as a store of value, citing central bank demand, market maturity and Bitcoin’s risk-asset behavior.

articles.headline: Three Bitcoin Binance charts reveal the setup behind the next big move
articles.excerpt: Binance data points to shifting liquidity flows and evolving trader positioning that may support Bitcoin’s next price move.

articles.headline: BlackRock launches staked Ethereum ETF offering ETH exposure and yield
articles.excerpt: BlackRock’s iShares Staked Ethereum Trust ETF will trade on the Nasdaq, offering spot exposure and staking income with a reduced 0.12% fee on the first $2.5 billion.

articles.headline: Stablecoins are becoming crypto’s largest wasted resource
articles.excerpt: Stablecoins move trillions each year but mostly sit unused, leaving a widespread inefficiency across crypto markets.

articles.headline: Ether funding rate flips negative: Are ETH bears back in control?
articles.excerpt: Bearish Ether derivatives data and slowing network use weigh on ETH price, even as developers plan for faster transactions and more flexible wallet fees.

articles.headline: CFTC chair opens prediction markets rulemaking to public comment
articles.excerpt: “This ends today,“ said CFTC Chair Michael Selig, who has been reiterating his position that the agency has exclusive jurisdiction overseeing prediction markets platforms.

articles.headline: FATF warns offshore crypto firms create money laundering and sanctions gaps
articles.excerpt: A new FATF report says crypto exchanges operating offshore can create gaps in AML enforcement, making it harder for regulators to track illicit activity.

articles.headline: South Korea plans to use AI for crypto tax enforcement
articles.excerpt: The National Tax Service has opened a procurement bid for a platform to analyze crypto trading data and flag potential tax evasion.

articles.headline: Utah set to block prediction markets as state-federal tensions rise
articles.excerpt: CFTC Chair Michael Selig has said that the agency has authority over prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket and warned it will defend that jurisdiction in court if challenged.

articles.headline: US prosecutors urge judge to deny Sam Bankman-Fried retrial bid: Report
articles.excerpt: Prosecutors say testimony from two former FTX executives cited by the defense does not qualify as newly discovered evidence, Bloomberg reported.

articles.headline: Ghana greenlights 11 crypto companies for regulatory sandbox
articles.excerpt: Ghana’s SEC could grant a full license to sandbox participants as early as six months, provided that their products are market-ready and that they tick all regulatory boxes.

articles.headline: FDIC chair says no deposit insurance for stablecoins under GENIUS Act
articles.excerpt: A proposed plan by the agency would ban “pass-through insurance“ for stablecoins by third parties in addition to the FDIC not insuring deposits under the law.

articles.headline: US Senate bill targets prediction markets on war and assassinations
articles.excerpt: Democratic Senator Adam Schiff introduced a bill to ban prediction markets related to war, death and terrorism amid escalating insider trading concerns related to military operations.

articles.headline: The Emperor has no wallet
articles.excerpt: Why crypto still hasn’t solved a single everyday problem, argues VerifiedX’s Pollak.

articles.headline: U.S. Senate votes to ban CBDCs in housing bill that may face trouble in…
articles.excerpt: A bipartisan housing bill was approved in the Senate, carrying an unrelated ban on U.S. central bank digital currencies, but its future remains uncertain.
